name: String DP — LCS, Edit Distance, Palindromic Subsequence

metadata.career_relevance: Edit distance powers Zerodha's typo correction in stock search. LCS underlies diff tools and version control. These 2D DP problems appear in senior engineering interviews at Flipkart, Amazon, and FAANG India.

hook.real_world_problem:
```
Zerodha stock search typo correction:
User types: 'RELINCE' (misspelled)
Correct: 'RELIANCE'

Edit distance: 2 operations (insert 'A', insert 'A')
Top matches by edit distance ≤ 3 shown as suggestions

Edit distance O(mn) DP:
  m=7, n=8: 56 cells
  For 100K stocks: 56 × 100K = 5.6M ops
  Fast enough for real-time suggestions
```
hook.what_you_will_build: LCS, Edit Distance, Longest Palindromic Subsequence — 3 core string DP patterns.

visual_aid.description: LCS DP table for s1='ABCBDAB' and s2='BDCAB'. 8×6 table (including row/col 0). Filled cell by cell: match diagonal+1, no-match max(above,left). Arrows show the optimal alignment traced back. Answer: dp[7][5]=4 (BCAB or BDAB). Second diagram: Edit distance table for 'horse' to 'ros'.
visual_aid.alt_text: LCS and edit distance DP tables with fill order

worked_example.problem_statement: Minimum operations (insert, delete, replace) to convert 'horse' to 'ros'. Answer: 3.

step_by_step_solution.details: dp[i][0]=i: delete all i chars. dp[0][j]=j: insert all j chars. Fill row by row.

worked_example.final_code: int[][] dp=new int[m+1][n+1]; for(int i=0;i<=m;i++)dp[i][0]=i; for(int j=0;j<=n;j++)dp[0][j]=j; for(int i=1;i<=m;i++) for(int j=1;j<=n;j++) dp[i][j]=s1.charAt(i-1)==s2.charAt(j-1)?dp[i-1][j-1]:1+Math.min(dp[i-1][j-1],Math.min(dp[i-1][j],dp[i][j-1]));
things_to_notice: dp[i][0]=i: need i deletions to go from s1[0..i-1] to empty
Three operations: replace (diagonal), delete (from above), insert (from left)
LCS and edit distance are related: editDist = m + n - 2×LCS for unit costs

industry_examples.use_case: Autocomplete and spell correction for stock tickers. Edit distance ≤ 2 → show suggestion. LCS-based diff for trade record comparison.

applications.interview_relevance: LCS and edit distance are the two canonical 2D string DP problems. Know: state definition, recurrence, base cases. Mention O(n) space optimization (rolling rows) for bonus points.
applications.real_world_workflow: String DP pattern: (1) define dp[i][j] clearly, (2) identify base cases (empty string), (3) write recurrence (match vs no-match), (4) fill bottom-up, (5) read answer from dp[m][n].

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Not clearly defining what dp[i][j] represents. For LCS: dp[i][j] = LCS of s1[0..i-1] and s2[0..j-1] — note the 1-indexing. dp[0][j]=0 (empty s1), dp[i][0]=0 (empty s2). Students often index from 0 without the +1 buffer row/column.
common_gaps.remediation: Always use size (m+1)×(n+1) for string DP. Row 0 = empty s1 (base case). Column 0 = empty s2. s1[0..i-1] represented by dp[i]. Index i means we've considered i characters of s1.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Student uses dp[m][n] array (not m+1, n+1) and gets ArrayIndexOutOfBounds or wrong answer

key_takeaways: Base cases: dp[0][j]=j (edit dist) or 0 (LCS). dp[i][0]=i or 0
Space O(n): only need previous row → rolling array optimization
Min insertions for palindrome: n - LPS(s) (= n - LCS(s, rev(s)))

self_check_questions.question: How are LCS and Edit Distance related?
self_check_questions.answer: For unit operation costs (insert, delete, replace all cost 1): editDistance(s1, s2) = m + n - 2×LCS(s1, s2). Intuition: the longest common subsequence represents characters we don't need to change. The remaining characters in s1 (m - LCS) need to be deleted, and the remaining in s2 (n - LCS) need to be inserted. That's (m-LCS) + (n-LCS) = m+n-2×LCS total operations. This is only for unit costs without replacement — Levenshtein distance with replacement has a different formula.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Module 31 complete! Next: M32 DSA4 — Linked Lists.
